response: Yes, both Daniel Mann and Todd Solondz were directors. Daniel Mann was an American film and television director, while Todd Solondz was an American independent film director known for his work in dark, socially conscious satire. Mann directed films such as "Fear, Anxiety & Depression" and "Life During Wartime," while Solondz directed films like "Welcome to the Dollhouse," "Storytelling," "Palindromes," and "Life During Wartime."
